name: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
applicable_scenarios: reduce dimensionality of continuous data while preserving variance
de-correlate / whiten features before downstream models
math_form: X = U \Sigma V^T, \; Z = X V_k
python_template:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA

def pca_fit(X, n_components=2):
    model = PCA(n_components=n_components)
    Z = model.fit_transform(X)
    return model, Z  # model.explained_variance_ratio_
typical_cases: feature compression before clustering or classification
noise reduction in image / signal data
common_pitfalls: must standardize features of different scales before PCA
components are linear; curved manifolds need kernel PCA or t-SNE / UMAP
components are not interpretable features — rotated loadings help
explained-variance threshold is domain-dependent; plot the scree
